verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a cytoplasmic chaperone at baseline, but translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under defined non-baseline conditions: oncogenic transformation (ecto-HSP70 display on tumor cells), heat shock and cellular stress, and immunogenic cell death. Cell-surface HSP70 is a validated therapeutic target (anti-HSP70 mAbs in clinical development). The protein body is accessible from the extracellular face when surface-localized, but only under these specific cellular states, not at baseline.
